steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Predict Context Timeout Ownership · Theory Context Timeout Ownership
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 1336 during a browser upload compatibility rollout. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to narrow retry outcomes and preserve AbortSignal and ambiguous-write reconciliation; bound bytes, time, attempts, promises, streams, concurrency, cache, and spend; then double concurrent Upload instances and preserve aggregate memory and worker budgets. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, compose request, attempt, transfer, and shutdown deadlines and propagate cancellation through body and async task ownership.
